speaker: pundit
text: He's caught that sweetly, right off the laces.
previousText: The crowd is still roaring as the replay of the goal rolls.
nextText: The celebrations settle and play restarts from the centre circle.

speaker: pundit
text: Look at the full back arriving late. Nobody picks him up.
previousText: The replay of the goal is rolling as the pundit weighs in.
nextText: Attention returns to the restart.

speaker: pundit
text: Top drawer, that. You don't save those.
previousText: The stadium is still buzzing after a fine strike.
nextText: The noise dies down as the teams line up again.

speaker: pundit
text: It all starts with one lazy clearance, and they're punished.
previousText: A second replay shows the build up to the goal.
nextText: Play is about to restart from the centre circle.

speaker: pundit
text: One lovely ball over the top and the defence is gone.
previousText: The goal is shown again from behind the play.
nextText: The commentator picks up the restart.

speaker: pundit
text: Simple goals, this game. Get it wide, get it in, gamble.
previousText: The pundit gives his view as the replay finishes.
nextText: The match resumes from the kick off.

speaker: pundit
text: He sets himself early, and that's why the save looks easy.
previousText: A fine save has just been shown again in replay.
nextText: The match settles back into its rhythm.

speaker: pundit
text: Quick feet across his line. The hands take care of themselves.
previousText: The replay lingers on the goalkeeper's stop.
nextText: Play continues from the resulting corner.

speaker: pundit
text: He shows him the outside, then takes it away. Clever keeping.
previousText: The pundit assesses the save as the replay runs.
nextText: Attention returns to the action on the pitch.

speaker: pundit
text: Watch his weight stay forward. That's textbook, that.
previousText: A slow motion replay picks out the goalkeeper's technique.
nextText: The game moves on with a goal kick.

speaker: pundit
text: In my day that's a fair shoulder, but the whistle's gone.
previousText: The referee has just blown for a foul.
nextText: Players regroup as the free kick is prepared.

speaker: pundit
text: He's gone for the ball and got the man. That's a foul.
previousText: The challenge is shown again from the touchline camera.
nextText: The free kick is lined up.

speaker: pundit
text: Soft, for me. They give those far too easily nowadays.
previousText: The pundit watches the replay of the challenge.
nextText: Play resumes with the free kick.

speaker: pundit
text: An honest challenge, just a fraction late. He'll know that himself.
previousText: The referee has a quiet word after the tackle.
nextText: The game restarts and tempers cool.

speaker: pundit
text: Whoever wins the midfield scrap usually wins the afternoon.
previousText: A lull in play gives the pundit a moment to assess.
nextText: Attention returns to the action on the pitch.

speaker: pundit
text: The far side full back is asleep there. Somebody must tell him.
previousText: The camera pans across the defensive line during a stoppage.
nextText: The commentator picks up the play once more.

speaker: pundit
text: They've gone man for man in midfield and it's a proper battle.
previousText: The pundit surveys the pattern of the game.
nextText: Play continues in the middle of the park.

speaker: pundit
text: Both banks of four are sitting deep. Somebody has to take risks.
previousText: A wide shot shows both teams holding their positions.
nextText: The ball is worked patiently across the pitch.

speaker: pundit
text: Watch the spare man in the middle. Nobody's picking him up.
previousText: The pundit spots something as play slows down.
nextText: The commentator returns to calling the action.

speaker: pundit
text: The wide men aren't tracking back, and that's where the trouble starts.
previousText: A break in play allows a word on the team shape.
nextText: The match picks up its pace again.

speaker: pundit
text: That's lovely stuff, one touch each and never a wasted pass.
previousText: A sweeping attacking move has just unfolded.
nextText: The commentator picks up the play once more.

speaker: pundit
text: Credit the winger there. He commits his man before releasing it.
previousText: The replay shows the attack developing down the flank.
nextText: Attention returns to the live action.

speaker: pundit
text: They've gone through that midfield like a knife through butter.
previousText: The crowd applauds a flowing attacking move.
nextText: Play continues at a high tempo.

speaker: pundit
text: Good old-fashioned wing play, that. Beat your man, get it across.
previousText: The pundit reflects on the attack as play slows.
nextText: The match settles back into its rhythm.

speaker: pundit
text: One side has bossed that half. The other needs a long talk.
previousText: The half time whistle has gone and the pundit gives his view.
nextText: Coverage heads to the interval break.

speaker: pundit
text: Honestly, you couldn't split them. A half of few clear chances.
previousText: The teams head down the tunnel at the interval.
nextText: The broadcast moves to the half time summary.

speaker: pundit
text: A scrappy old half, lots of effort, not much quality.
previousText: The half time whistle brings a pause in the coverage.
nextText: The interval analysis continues in the studio.

speaker: pundit
text: End to end, that. Both managers will want it tightened up.
previousText: The players walk off after a lively opening half.
nextText: Coverage heads to the interval break.

speaker: pundit
text: Over the piece, the better side has come out on top.
previousText: The final whistle has blown and the pundit gives his verdict.
nextText: The broadcast moves to the closing summary.

speaker: pundit
text: A fair result, for me. Neither keeper had a quiet afternoon.
previousText: The players shake hands as the match ends.
nextText: The commentary team wraps up the coverage.

speaker: pundit
text: They've ground that out the hard way, and they won't care a bit.
previousText: The final whistle sounds after a hard fought finish.
nextText: The broadcast moves to the closing summary.

speaker: pundit
text: Not one for the scrapbook, but the points are what matter.
previousText: The match ends and the pundit offers a final word.
nextText: The commentary team signs off.

speaker: pundit
text: Forget the keeper, pick your corner and hit it true.
previousText: A penalty has been awarded and tension fills the ground.
nextText: The taker places the ball on the spot.

speaker: pundit
text: All the pressure is on the taker, and he knows it.
previousText: The crowd whistles as the penalty is prepared.
nextText: The referee waits for the area to clear.

speaker: pundit
text: He's gone too soon. A yard of patience and he's clean through.
previousText: The flag has gone up for offside.
nextText: The free kick is taken and play moves on.

speaker: pundit
text: Fair play to the linesman, that's a tight one to spot.
previousText: The replay checks the offside decision.
nextText: Play resumes with the free kick.

speaker: pundit
text: He talks his back four through the whole ninety. You can't coach that.
previousText: The commentator has just asked about the goalkeeper.
nextText: Talk returns to the match in progress.

speaker: pundit
text: Crosses are his bread and butter. He comes and claims everything.
previousText: The camera lingers on the goalkeeper as the question is asked.
nextText: Attention returns to the action on the pitch.

speaker: pundit
text: Nothing fancy. Heads it, kicks it, and the winger gets no change.
previousText: The commentator has just asked about the defender.
nextText: Talk returns to the match in progress.

speaker: pundit
text: He reads the game well. The first tackle is rarely needed.
previousText: The defender is picked out by the camera during a lull.
nextText: The commentary returns to the live play.

speaker: pundit
text: He does the running nobody notices. Every side needs one of those.
previousText: The commentator has just asked about the midfielder.
nextText: Talk returns to the match in progress.

speaker: pundit
text: Give him a yard and he'll find the forward's feet every time.
previousText: The midfielder is the subject of the broadcast chat.
nextText: The commentary picks up the action again.

speaker: pundit
text: He only needs one chance. Defenders hate playing against that sort.
previousText: The commentator has just asked about the forward.
nextText: Talk returns to the match in progress.

speaker: pundit
text: He works the channels well and brings others into the play.
previousText: The forward is discussed during a quiet spell.
nextText: Attention returns to the action on the pitch.

speaker: pundit
text: Early tackles set the tone in these games. Expect a few.
previousText: Before kick off, the commentator asks for the pundit's view.
nextText: The teams make their way out for kick off.

speaker: pundit
text: The pitch looks quick, so the team that passes it should enjoy today.
previousText: The camera sweeps across the ground before the start.
nextText: The referee checks his watch ahead of kick off.

speaker: pundit
text: For me, it comes down to who wants the second ball more.
previousText: The pre-match discussion turns to how the game might go.
nextText: The players complete their final warm up.

speaker: pundit
text: Both benches will preach patience, but the crowd won't allow it for long.
previousText: The pundit offers a final thought before the whistle.
nextText: The teams make their way out for kick off.

speaker: pundit
text: There are no points at stake, but players are still fighting for their places.
previousText: Before kickoff, the pundit looks down at the field.
nextText: It should be an open game.

speaker: pundit
text: A good chance for both teams to build fitness and test their tactical setups.
previousText: The pundit shares his thoughts on the exhibition match.
nextText: Managers will want a clean sheet.

speaker: pundit
text: In a short group stage, getting a good start is absolutely vital.
previousText: Before the match begins, the pundit evaluates the group stage.
nextText: A loss here puts you under massive pressure.

speaker: pundit
text: It's win or go home now. One mistake could send a nation packing.
previousText: The pundit looks ahead to the knockout stage.
nextText: No room for errors today.

speaker: pundit
text: We are reaching the business end now. Only eight teams remain.
previousText: The pundit previews the international quarter-final.
nextText: Every single player will feel the pressure.

speaker: pundit
text: A nation's hopes rest on this game. The pressure must be immense.
previousText: The pundit focuses on the semi-final tension.
nextText: Only ninety minutes separate them from the final.

speaker: pundit
text: This is the ultimate game. Winning today writes your name into history.
previousText: The pundit gazes at the cup final crowd.
nextText: The champions will be crowned today.

speaker: pundit
text: Cup football is all about who performs on the day. League form goes out the window.
previousText: The pundit gives his verdict on this cup match.
nextText: Expect a proper cup battle.

speaker: pundit
text: Getting this close to the final makes teams cautious. Expect a cagey start.
previousText: The pundit previews the domestic cup quarter-final.
nextText: Neither side will want to concede early.

speaker: pundit
text: This is the cruelest round to lose. You can feel the nerves out there.
previousText: The pundit discusses the semi-final atmosphere.
nextText: It comes down to who handles the nerves.

speaker: pundit
text: Legends are made in finals. These players will remember today forever.
previousText: The pundit looks at the trophy before kickoff.
nextText: A special afternoon awaits us.

speaker: pundit
text: At this stage of the campaign, every single point counts.
previousText: The pundit assesses the league fixture.
nextText: A draw is rarely enough.

speaker: pundit
text: The league table doesn't lie, and today's result will have a major impact.
previousText: The pundit looks at the current standings.
nextText: A big ninety minutes ahead.

speaker: pundit
text: Two of the league's heavyweights going head-to-head. A real six-pointer.
previousText: The pundit discusses the top of the table clash.
nextText: This is the match everyone wanted to see.

speaker: pundit
text: Neither team can afford to lose this. It will be a tense, scrappy match.
previousText: The pundit previews the relegation battle.
nextText: Expect plenty of commitment in the tackle.

speaker: pundit
text: On paper it looks like an easy win for the favorites, but surprises happen every week.
previousText: The pundit evaluates the gap between the sides.
nextText: The underdogs will relish this.
